en.extract:
The western yellow-billed barbet (Trachylaemus goffinii) is a species of bird in the African barbet family Lybiidae that is found from Sierra Leone to southwest Nigeria in West Africa. The species was formerly considered to be conspecific with the eastern yellow-billed barbet.


Taxonomy
The western yellow-billed barbet was formally described in 1863 by the Dutch army officer and ornithologist Andreas Leopold August Goffin (1837–1863) under the binomial name Capito goffinii. The specimens had been collected by Cornelis Nagtglas in the Dutch Gold Coast (now part of Ghana). The western yellow-billed barbet is now placed with the eastern yellow-billed barbet in the genus Trachylaemus that was introduced in 1891 by the German ornithologist Anton Reichenow. This species was formerly considered to be conspecific with the eastern yellow-billed barbet with the common name "yellow-billed barbet". The western yellow-billed barbet is now considered to be a separate species based on the differences in morphology and the deep mitochondrial DNA divergence.
Two subspecies are recognised:

T. g. goffinii (Goffin, 1863) – Sierra Leone to Ghana
T. g. togoensis (Reichenow, 1891) – Togo to southwest Nigeria


Notes


References

The '''western yellow-billed barbet''' ('''''Trachylaemus goffinii''''') is a species of bird in the African barbet family [[Lybiidae]] that is found from Sierra Leone to southwest Nigeria in [[West Africa]]. The species was formerly considered to be [[conspecific]] with the [[eastern yellow-billed barbet]].

==Taxonomy==
The western yellow-billed barbet was [[species description|formally described]] in 1863 by the Dutch army officer and ornithologist Andreas Leopold August Goffin (1837–1863) under the [[binomial name]] ''Capito goffinii''. The specimens had been collected by [[Cornelis Nagtglas]] in the [[Dutch Gold Coast]] (now part of Ghana).<ref>{{ cite book | last=Goffin | first=Andreas Leopold August | editor-last=Schlegel | editor-first=Hermann | editor-link=Hermann Schlegel | date=1863 | title=Revue Méthodique et Critique des Collections Déposées dans cet Établissement | volume=1: Monograph 15 Buccones | location=Leiden | publisher=Rijksmuseum van Natuurlijke Historie | page=72-73 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14871643 }}</ref>{{efn|Although Goffin lists the species as "''Capito goffinii'' Schlegel, in Mus. Batav.", there is no evidence that [[Hermann Schlegel]] was responsible for the description.<ref>{{ cite book | last1=van den Hoek Ostende | first1=L.W. | last2=Dekker | first2=R.W.R.J. | last3=Keijl | first3=G.O. | date=1997 | title=Type‐specimens of birds in the National Museum of Natural History, Leiden | series=Technical Bulletin, 1 | location=Leiden | publisher=Nationaal Natuurhistorisch Museum | page=200 | url=https://repository.naturalis.nl/pub/216191/TB01.pdf }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor1-last=Dickinson | editor1-first=E.C. | editor1-link=Edward C. Dickinson | editor2-last=Remsen | editor2-first=J.V. Jr. | editor2-link=James Van Remsen Jr. | year=2013 | title=The Howard & Moore Complete Checklist of the Birds of the World | volume=1: Non-passerines | edition=4th | location=Eastbourne, UK | publisher=Aves Press | isbn=978-0-9568611-0-8 | page=330 |url=https://archive.org/details/howardmoorecompl0001howa/page/330/mode/1up | url-access=registration }}</ref>}} The western yellow-billed barbet is now placed with the [[eastern yellow-billed barbet]] in the genus ''[[Trachylaemus]]'' that was introduced in 1891 by the German ornithologist [[Anton Reichenow]].<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | date=February 2025 | title=Jacamars, puffbirds, toucans, barbets, honeyguides | work=IOC World Bird List Version 15.1 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/jacamars/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=26 March 2025}}</ref> This species was formerly considered to be [[conspecific]] with the [[eastern yellow-billed barbet]] with the common name "yellow-billed barbet". The western yellow-billed barbet is now considered to be a separate species based on the differences in morphology and the deep [[mitochondrial]] DNA [[genetic divergence|divergence]].<ref name=ioc/>

Two [[subspecies]] are recognised:<ref name=ioc/>
* ''T. g. goffinii'' (Goffin, 1863) – Sierra Leone to Ghana
* ''T. g. togoensis'' ([[Anton Reichenow|Reichenow]], 1891) – Togo to southwest Nigeria
